way line from the intersection OF
Mill street and Main avenue
down Mill to Front, on Front to
Howard, and on Howard to Mallon
avenue, and thence west on Mallon
avenue, asking permission to cross
the bridge over the Spokane river
on Howard.


1--WE hereby offer to the city
council, if such franchise is grant.
ed, to pay the city the sum Of &c..
900 toward building new bridge
over the south channel and to-
wards the Strengthening and such
other repairs as may be necessary
to the other bridges on the street.


There are no provisions in the
proposed ordinance in respect to
this matter, but in its deliberations
upon the ordinance the city council
may insert such provisions in ref
erence to this matter as in It's wis
dom appears BEST"


Some interested parties got out
a dodger this morning asking that
citizens go to the city hall this
evening and impress the council
with the fact that the Washington
Water Power company has owned
Spokane long enough and must be
compelled to live up to its agree
ments with the people.
